tweets.text:
Sucks to see EUropooors losing access to global CEXs. 

When OKx closed my global account  to the OKx EU I stopped using it altogether. 

Because many of the tokens I wanted to trade were not on the EU version. And liquidity was worse. 

Before that it was my main CEX together with Binance. 

Now, Bybit doing the same. 

So at the end, the EU regulation makes products worse for me.

tweets.text:
The Supreme Court will rule on birthright citizenship tomorrow morning…

It’s not an exaggeration to say this could be the most important decision in our lifetimes.

Under the current interpretation of the 14th Amendment, a foreign citizen of another nation can cross our border illegally at 9 months pregnant, give birth to an American citizen, and then return home. Their child is now eligible for US benefits and can vote in our elections.

The Chinese can also pay to have surrogate children on US territorial islands in the Pacific, then immediately ship the babies to China to be raised under the CCP. Those children are now eligible for US benefits and can vote in our elections.

Both of these scenarios are happening. It’s simply wrong and not at all what the Amendment was intended for, which was former slaves and their children during Reconstruction.

If it stands, US citizenship means nothing.

tweets.text:
Yes.  Most are satisfied with read the only difference is rapidity of response on question from as fast as possible for alpha to second priority but assured within 24 hours for read. Usually pretty immediate anyway.  

OTOH if you know you are going to be high maintenance choose alpha. If not choose read and if you need more attention or are turn out to be high maintenance we can adjust.

tweets.text:
Crypto used to be amazing in storytelling

From digital gold to NFTs as cultural assets

But with AI sucking out financial liquidity from all sectors, no other crypto story is strong enough to attract inflows.

So we need this story telling creativity to come back to push AI <> Crypto narrative beyond CT.

- DePin/Onchain GPUs
- Agentic payments with stablecoins
- Proof of Authenticity
- Tokenized AI models
- Proof of Humanity

So much potential and some proved use cases but we got worse in story telling. 

Got lost with BTC is crypto thus everything else follows BTC price.

thread_tweets.text:
The 2017 (ICO) and 2021(Defi, DAOs, NFTs) were mostly narrative plays and we ended up building real things from these innovations.

What I am saying is that we can make up great stories which are inflated, sure, but attract capital, attention and builders to chase those dreams.

ICOs were empty white papers but they attracted capital and we got some of those ideas implemented: e.g., prediction markets.

tweets.text:
I'd tend to side with @jukan05 here:

- Power keeps the data centre alive
- Compute thinks
- Memory lets the agent remain itself

People are underestimating the potential of persistent memory; it's THE most important "sub-theme" coming out of the AI trade

Yes memory stocks (and now some altcoin memory tokens) have pumped, but that doesn't mean there isn't further to run

Pay attention to AI memory quality; to coin an Animal Farm quote:

"All memory is equal, but some memory is more equal than others"

I'll be dropping an article on AI memory in my free newsletter (link in my bio) so turn notis on + subscribe

Last article on AI inference will also be useful context, i've linked below
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Yes, MU is not Nvidia.

But going forward, it may become even more important than Nvidia.

Think about it. Inference is now directly tied to money. But inference does not get better simply by adding more Nvidia GPUs. In fact, GPUs are often underutilized in inference, sitting

I'd tend to side with @jukan05 here:

- Power keeps the data centre alive
- Compute thinks
- Memory lets the agent remain itself

People are underestimating the potential of persistent memory; it's THE most important "sub-theme" coming out of the AI trade

Yes memory stocks (and now some altcoin memory tokens) have pumped, but that doesn't mean there isn't further to run

Pay attention to AI memory quality; to coin an Animal Farm quote:

"All memory is equal, but some memory is more equal than others"

I'll be dropping an article on AI memory in my free newsletter (link in my bio) so turn notis on + subscribe

tweets.text:
“Samsung and SKHynix to invest a trillion”
“Elon says production has to increase”
“Tim cook has never seen anything like it”

“That’s bullish” 

Quite the opposite. 

1) Capex increase 
2) Memory supply expansion 

If anything, extortion by capping supply on a commodity, debeers style, was their best bet. Although, they couldn’t have kept that going forever. 

1) Customers would invest in alternatives 
2) Competitors would arise 
3) Memory supply expansion anyway 

The tighter the bottleneck, the greater the incentive to widen it.

tweets.text:
Strategy recently announced its BTC Monetization Program, a framework that authorizes the company to sell bitcoin to fund the preferred dividend bill and finance preferred buybacks.

Our STRC report traced how the obligation behaves under pressure. While bitcoin appreciates, common equity carries the preferred dividends. Once mNAV falls below 1x, that channel stops working. Strategy is there now.

The cost moves down the structure. Common holders absorbed it first, through the dilution that funded the preferred dividends. The reserve and bitcoin sales come next. Strategy already tested that in late May, selling 32 coins to cover STRC distributions. The new framework makes it routine.

Facing this, Strategy had three options available: repurchase STRC, sell bitcoin, or suspend the dividend. It reached for two, authorizing buybacks and bitcoin sales, and rejected the last by raising the dividend rather than cutting it.

Strategy holds roughly 17 months of coverage in cash, so abandoning STRC would not require selling bitcoin today. The decision to authorize sales signals the opposite, that the 12% dividend remains funded and the channel stays open.

To keep the credit stack credible, Strategy will spend the asset the stack was built to accumulate. The bet was always about surviving long enough to be right.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Strategy announces a Digital Credit Capital Framework designed to strengthen Digital Credit, enhance liquidity, preserve long-term Bitcoin exposure, and support long-term value creation. $MSTR $STRC
https://t.co/P770rd7fva

tweets.text:
Gm ☕️

Lots of growth and labour market data this week to look forward to before Friday — as per MMM regime modelling I think the most probable move is back towards Goldilocks from Overheat over coming months as inflation impulses have collapsed whilst growth impulses have bottomed (and despite the noise liquidity impulses have v-reversed from bottom decile to top decile over the past couple of months)

Writing up this week’s Market Outlook, which will be available shortly at @Ostium and then later on X and Substack

Will share more detailed thoughts in there 🤝

tweets.text:
In addition to a 99th percentile reading in the liquidity nowcast, there has never been an instance of both the liquidity impulse + liquidity composite being above the 75th percentile together - this is a first (in ~10 years of data I have for both)

Impulse drives the composite on a longer-term time horizon - this is usually why there is a negative contemporaneous correlation between the two

It is significantly more probable that 'liquidity' is higher not lower into 2027 and through that year imo - and I expect that when the *impulse* tops out and rolls over we will see that reflected in growing risk appetite over the subsequent 12-18 months 

(You can run your own studies like these using the regime data at https://t.co/oljeEa7GpB)
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Liquidity Nowcast 

This is now in the 99th percentile since the 1990s - surpassed only by mid-2020

(This is a live reading so subject to change - my modelling does not use live readings for strategies fwiw)

https://t.co/9uHhq1SVtf https://t.co/xtLlBuoiQt

tweets.text:
$GM cuts 1,000 workers and replaces them with 50 robots (Jun 22nd report)

Reports also show GM is working on a deal with $NVDA on factory robotics.

This is industry validation for robotics, since it likely demonstrates increased opex margins + efficiency from employee automation. 

Especially from companies like $AMZN to General Motors with assembly/warehouse lines that have high headcount.

I think the technology is already here and use cases are already proven. Robotics/Humanoids are just pre-scale right now. 

The wording from large companies is “assistive robotics”… But in all honesty, it’s here to automate away the human workforce and increase profitability.

I don’t think companies want to be paying insurance, salaries, etc. when robots can do a better job, and 24/7, so very sad to see potential job losses in the future from robotics automation.

Whether people like it or not; seems like the inflection point for robotics is here.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Robotics is next. 

Both deal count and investment amounts are skyrocketing per pitchbook March data (source: a16z)

Good thing is: the same AI DC exposure often has cross-exposure to humanoid ramp.

Like DRAM/NAND with memory (on humanoid inference/storage) or DFB lasers with https://t.co/7BrzCd3cWD

tweets.text:
been talking about prop trading for a while now, how your downside is capped by design while your upside isn't.

this is a perfect example. The maximum you could spend on evaluation fees is ~ $1,000 and one trader turned that into a $30,000 payout from a single withdrawal.

If you know how to manage risk, prop trading can be an incredible tool for scaling your capital.

right now, the cost to get funded at @breakoutprop is the lowest I've seen. I'm not sure how long it'll last, so if you've been considering it, now's a good time to lock in your account size before pricing goes back up.

get funded:
https://t.co/T4EF0FHzhU

You can pushback on the concept of rain generally or you can build models to try and predict the weather :D

You know what I mean, its not ideal but we need to deal with the reality that presents itself to us

Surely if the winning model is that all revenue = owner revenue, markets will converge there, without us pushing back on dashboards that try to give us precise data today

tweets.text:
Labor needs to participate in ownership in order to resolve this problem. The best companies understand this and spread equity broadly throughout their workforces. 

Worth a careful read.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
⚡️The worker has become the liquidity layer for capital.

Labor does the work, absorbs the schedule, carries the stress, pays the tax immediately, rents the shelter, buys the insurance, services the debt, and gets told the economy is healthy because the asset owners are richer.
tweets.full_text:
Labor needs to participate in ownership in order to resolve this problem. The best companies understand this and spread equity broadly throughout their workforces. 

Worth a careful read.
